# Code of Conduct

We aim to make our projects welcoming, respectful, and safe for all participants. This Code of Conduct describes the behaviors we expect, what crosses the line, how to report concerns, and how we'll address them.

## Where this applies

This policy covers all project spaces: issue trackers, pull requests, mailing lists, chat channels, and in-person events when you're representing the project.

We are dedicated to fostering an inclusive, harassment-free environment for contributors, maintainers, and anyone taking part in the project.

## Expected and unacceptable behavior

### Expected behavior

- Use respectful, considerate language and actions.
- Assume good intent and provide feedback that is constructive and actionable.
- Welcome newcomers and offer help when you can.
- Keep conversations focused and on-topic to solve problems.

### Unacceptable behavior

The following are examples of behavior that will not be tolerated:

- Harassment, threats, or intimidation of any kind.
- Discrimination or hateful language directed at protected characteristics.
- Personal attacks, insults, or sharing private information (doxxing).
- Deliberate disruption, trolling, or spamming.
- Retaliation against someone who raises a concern.

## How to report

If you experience or see behavior that violates this Code of Conduct, contact the project maintainers at [community@skift.work](mailto:community@skift.work). If you’d rather report anonymously, use our anonymous form: https://whistle.skift.work/.

When filing a report, include what happened, when and where it occurred, and any relevant links or screenshots. You don’t need to provide proof — tell us what you observed.

## Enforcement and follow-up

Reports will be reviewed carefully and handled confidentially. Possible responses include a private warning, temporary or permanent removal from project spaces, or other appropriate actions.

Decisions are made by the project maintainers or appointed moderators. If you’re unhappy with how a report was dealt with, contact [community@skift.work](mailto:community@skift.work) to raise your concerns.

## Tone and intent

We encourage a friendly, inclusive tone, but the need to enforce this Code of Conduct may require firm measures in some situations — our priority is a safe, productive community for everyone.

---

_If you have questions about this policy or need assistance, email [community@skift.work](mailto:community@skift.work)._
